description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, black, crescent-shaped blades on either side of a central wooden shaft, each blade curving outward with pointed tips. The shaft is reinforced with multiple black grip sections, likely made of textured metal or rubber, providing secure handling. At the bottom end of the shaft, there is a spiked mace-like pommel, adding both weight and a secondary striking function. The overall construction suggests a blend of wood and metal, with the dark blades and spiked pommel giving it a menacing, medieval appearance. The object is visually striking, with a mix of matte and slightly glossy finishes on the metal parts.
description: A double-headed battle axe with black crescent blades, a reinforced wooden shaft, and a spiked pommel. The design is robust and visually intimidating, blending wood and metal elements.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing both large black blades, the central shaft, and the spiked pommel clearly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the shaft, with the spiked pommel visible at the bottom.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both blades and the shaft with the spiked pommel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and shaft, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with multiple black textured grip sections, likely rubber or metal, providing enhanced grip and control.
attribute_blade_design: Double crescent-shaped blades with pointed tips, symmetrical, no beard, and a central mounting; blades are robust and visually intimidating.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible integration of blade and shaft, reinforced by grip sections.
